ety: v.n. fr. فجل 'to be thick, or flaccid'
def: s.m. A radish, Raphanus sativus; — the heel; — adj. Thick.
more: 1. s. m. A radish. 2. The heel. 3. adj. Thick.

def: s.m. Wickedness; immorality; adultery; lust.
s.m. pl. (of faqīh), Lawyers; theologians.
s.m. pl. (of faqīr), The poor; religious mendicants, derwishes.
s.f. Steel
more: n. m. Steel. faulādī , adj. Made of steel. faulād kā kushtah , n. Calcined steel. faulād kā arq , n. Tincture of steel.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian فولَاد (fōlād).

def: adj. Of steel, steel-; — s.f. A pike-staff.
more: 1. adj. Made of steel. 2. s. f. A pikestaff.

def: adj. & s.m. Such a; such and such; a certain (man); — such a man, such a one; so and so; such a thing; — the unmentionable thing or part, penis virilis.
more: (vulg. falān, Heb. from To lie hid) adj. Such a one or thing. ¶ falān or فلاني falānī, s. f. ( met. ) Pudendum muliebre.

def: adj. pron. A certain person or thing; such a one. 1. Shaksh-i-falāṅ . 2. Falāṅ bāt . 3. Kahne lagī, ki falānā ādmī yūṅ kahtā thā. falān , n. m. The organs of generation. falān-marānī , n. f. A woman who prostitutes herself for pleasure, in contradistinction to peṭ marānī , q. v.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian فلان (fulān), from Arabic فُلَان (fulān).

ety: for A. فلانة fulānat, fem. of fulān
def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ī) = fulān, q.v.; — falānī, s.f. Pudendum muliebre. adj. & s.m. Such a; such and such; a certain (man); — such a man, such a one; so and so; such a thing; — the unmentionable thing or part, penis virilis.
more: (or vulg. falāna ) Such a one, such a, a certain. ¶ falāna, s. m. Penis virilis.

def: s.m. (pl. of fals, q.v.; but used as a sing. also), Small copper coins; — a copper coin, a paisa (Ang. 'pice'); money.
more: n. m. A copper coin; a pice ( paisā ). Yak fallūs, va yak bād-kash, va qadre shīrīnī, va yak surāhī Sirī Krishn ārpan mediham . Kāyaths' play. A satire on the proneness of half-witted Persian and Arabic scholars to foist in learned words with the vernacular Hindī.
s. plur. m. (of فلس) Small coins, a paisā.

ety: an arabicised form; also bunduq, 'a ball, bullet'; 'a nut' (whence prob. bundūq or bandūq, 'a gun'); old forms are fendak, pendak; prob. akin to S. piṇṇa, or piṇḍaka, 'a ball'
def: s.f. A hazel-nut; — (met.) the stained tips of the fingers or toes (as being likened to hazel-nuts)
more: n. f. 1. The filbert nut. 2. ( Met .) The tips of the fingers or toes stained and resembling filberts.
s. f. The filbert nut; ( met. ) The tips of the fingers or toes stained and resembling those nuts. فندق یند finduḳ-band, Staining the tips of the fingers, &c. to resemble the filbert nut.
subs: s.f. Staining the tips of the fingers, &c. (to resemble the hazel-nut).

def: s.m. pl. (of fann, or fan), Ways, modes, varieties, diversities (rare); — arts, sciences; — arts, tricks, wiles, &c.
s.m. Name of a king of Kannauj (killed by Alexander the Great), Porus.
more: n. prop. Name of a king of Ḳannauj, killed by Alexander the Great. (Perhaps, Porus).

ety: for A. فرادَ furāda, quasi-plur. of fard, q.v.
def: adj. & adv. Sole, single, separate, solitary, unique; — singly, separately, one by one, one at a time, one after another
more: plur. (of فرد) Alone, single, so litary.

def: (v.n. fr. فرت 'to be sweet,' &c.), s.m. lit. 'Sweet, or thirst-subduing' (water); the river Euphrates.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian فرات (furāt), from Arabic الْفُرَات (al-furāt), ultimately from Akkadian 𒀀𒇉𒌓𒄒𒉣 (^(ÍD)Purattu).

ety: inf. n. of فرق; see farq
def: s.m. (orig. 'Anything that makes a separation or distinction between truth and falsity'; (hence) the Qǒrʼān.
more: (from فرق as distinguishing truth from falsehood) s. m. The ḳurʼān (Alcoran).

ety: for A. فرقة furqat, v.n. fr. فارق 'to separate oneself from,' &c., iii of فرق; see farq
def: s.f. Separation, disunion; abandonment; absence (of a beloved person); — distinction; distance ( = farq).
more: s. f. Distance, separation, distinction, absence (of lovers, &c.)

ety: for furoḵẖ, by change of خ to غ; see furoz, and furoḵẖta
def: s.m. Illumination, light, brightness, splendour; flame; — glory, fame, honour
more: n. m. Brightness (رونق 2). farog pānā, yā h. , v. n. To get name, honor, etc.
subs: v.n. To get glory, or honour, &c.
v.n. To become famous, or illustrious, &c.

ety: of furoḵẖtan = afroḵẖtan; rt. Zend ruć with prep. aiwi or aibi = S. अभि+रुच् (रोचति)
def: Kindled, inflamed; burnt; — shining, radiant ( = afroḵẖta).
more: part. pass. (of فروختن) Burnt, kindled, inflamed.

ety: rt. of furoḵẖtan = afroḵẖtan; see furoḵẖta
def: part. adj. Illumining, enlightening, kindling, inflaming (used as last member of compounds, e.g. dil-furoz, 'heart-inflaming'); — s.m. Splendour, brilliance, &c.
more: 1. part. act. (of فروختن) in compos. Kindling, inflaming, enlightening. 2. s. Splendour. فروز کرنا furoz karnā, To illuminate.

def: part. (of furoḵẖtan; see furoḵẖta, and furoz), Shining, luminous, resplendent; inflaming.
more: (part. act. of فروختن) adj. Inflaming, shining.

ety: for A. فرصة, v.n. fr. فرص 'to slit, cut, divide,' &c.
def: s.f. A time, opportunity, occasion; freedom (from), leisure; convenience; relief, recovery; respite, reprieve; rest, ease; (local) leave
more: n. f. فرص slit. See فراغت and سوفته 1. Leisure. 2. E. Leave ( chhuṭṭī ). 3. Recovery; relief. fursat chāhnā, yā māṅgnā , E. v. a. To ask leave. fursat denā , v. a. 1. To allow time, occasion, or opportunity for. 2. To give leave. fursat milnā, yā pānā , v. n. 1. To have time or leisure for; to be at leisure. 2. To obtain leave. 3. To be discharged, dismissed. fursat meṅ , adv. At one's leisure or convenience; leisurely. fursat h. , v. n. 1. To be at leisure. 2. To recover from illness. fursat yā mauqe ko hāth se na denā . To lose no opportunity; strike while the iron is hot; to make hay while the sun shines.
(from فرص) s. f. Leisure, freedom, ease, convenience, opportunity, occasion, time, relief, rest.
subs: or furṣat milnā, v.n. To find or get time or leisure (for); to be at leisure; to find or get relief or respite (from, -se); to be dismissed or discharged (from); to obtain leave
(-ko), To grant or allow time or opportunity (for); to afford relief (to), to respite, reprieve; to give or grant leave (to)
adv. At (one's) leisure, or convenience, leisurely
(-ko), To have leisure, be at leisure; to be relieved, to recover (from illness).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian فُرْصَت (fursat), borrowed from Arabic فُرْصَة (furṣa). First attested in c. 1503 as Middle Hindi فرصت (frṣt).

def: s.f. pl. (of فرع farʻ, q.v.), Tops, summits, heads; branches; subdivisions; ramifications; derivatives; — the derivative institutes of the law; fundamental principles or doctrines (of law or religion); — nobles, men of eminence (of a people or party), chiefs (of tribes or families).
more: s. plur. (of فرع) Tops, summits, branches, heads or chiefs of a family.

def: s.f. pl. (of furūʻ), Summits; branches; ramifications ( = furūʻ, q.v.).
adj. Branch; collateral, indirect; additional; — s.f. Income or revenue other than that from a fixed source, additional income.
s.m. pl. (of farsh, q.v.), Carpets, floor-cloths, &c.
s.m. pl. (of firāsh), Carpets; beds.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian فرش (farš), from Arabic فَرْش (farš, “carpet, rug”).

r: fushurda, fashurda, fushurdan, fashārdan, afshārdan, afshurdan, fashār
ety: perf. part. of fushurdan = fashārdan = afshārdan = afshurdan; see fashār
def: part. Squeezed, pressed, compressed; wrung
more: part. pass. (of فشردن) Squeezed, pressed. فشرده جگر fushurda-jigar, Squeezed heart, afflicted.
subs: adj. Having the heart wrung, afflicted.

ety: = afsūn, q.v. prob. akin to afsāna, q.v.
def: s.m. Enchantment, incantation, fascination, &c.
subs: adj. & s.m. Enchanting, fascinating, bewitching, &c.; — an enchanter, sorcerer, &c.

def: part. adj. = afsurda, q.v. part. adj. Frozen, frigid, benumbed; withered, faded; dispirited, dejected, low-spirited, melancholy
more: part. pass. (of فسردن) Frozen, withered, dejected. See افسرده afsurda.
subs: adj. Depressed in mind, dispirited, afflicted — afsurda-dil, adj. Fainthearted, low-spirited, dejected.

def: s.f. = afsurdagī, q.v. s.f. Frozenness; frigidity, coldness; numbness; dejection, melancholy, lowness or depression of spirits.
s.m. A foot (of measure).
wety: Borrowed from English foot.

def: s.f. pl. (of fatḥ, q.v.), Victories, conquests; — gains, pickings, perquisites; income received gratuitously; a gratuitous supply (of sustenance, &c.); abundance, easy circumstances.
more: n. f. Pl. of فتح q. v. Gains; pickings; perquisites.
s. f. plur. (of فتح) 1. Victories. 2. ( met. ) Abundance, easy circumstances, income received gratuitously.

def: s.f. pl. (of futūḥ), Numerous victories or conquests.

ety: inf. n. of فتر 'to abate'; 'to become languid,' &c.
def: s.m. Languor, weakness, infirmity; defect, imperfection, unsoundness; irregularity; — coolness; discord, quarrelling, quarrel, row, riot
more: n. m. فتر weakness. 1. Defect; unsoundness. 2. Row; riot ( khalal , 3, 5). Baiṭhe baiṭhe futūr uṭhāyā hai, Kuchh quzā kā payām āyā hai . Wom. Shauq. futūr barpā k., ḍālnā, yā k. , v. a. 1. To make a row. 2. To disorganize; sow dissension. futūr-i-aql . Unsound mind. futūr-i-hazm , n. m. Indigestion; dyspepsia ( an-pach ).
(from فتر) s. m. 1. Weakness, infirmity. 2. (Hind.) Quarrelling. ¶ fitūr (dakh.) Treachery, deceit, artifice, fraud, contrivance.
subs: or futūr ḍālnā, or futūr karnā (-meṅ), To create a disturbance, cause a quarrel, to sow discord (between)
s.m. Unsoundness of intellect or mind
s.m. Badness of digestion; indigestion, dyspepsia.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian فتور (futūr), from Arabic فُتُور (futūr). Cognate with Bengali ফিতুর (phitur).

def: adj. & s.m. Dispersed; ruined, undone; — exciting quarrels; contentious, litigious; factious; riotous; — a fomenter of quarrels or disturbances, a contentious or litigious person, &c.
adj. & s.m. Dispersed; ruined, undone; — exciting quarrels; contentious, litigious; factious; riotous; — a fomenter of quarrels or disturbances, a contentious or litigious person, &c.
more: n. m. A fomentor of disturbances ( āg-lagāū , 2).
adj. 1. Dispersed, ruined, undone. 2. (Hind.) Exciting quarrels.

def: s.f. Generosity, honourableness, liberality, bountifulness.
s.m. pl. (of faẓīl = fāẓil, q.v.), The excellent; virtuous or good men; learned men, the learned.
more: s. plur. (of فاضل) The excellent, the learned.

ety: for A. فضالة fuẓālat, v.n. fr. فضل; see faẓl
def: s.m. A redundant portion; remaining portion, remains, remainder.
more: (from فضل) s. m. Remains, remainder. (Plur. فضلات).

def: adj. = afzūd, q.v. s.m. Increase, addition, enlargement; abundance; — adj. More, greater, additional, superfluous, in excess
more: contract. of افزود afzūd, q. v.
subs: (-se), To be more (than), to exceed, surpass, &c.

ety: inf. n. of فصل 'to exceed'; or pl. of faẓl, but used as a sing.
def: s.m. Excess, redundance, &c. ( = faẓl); violence; a busy, meddling spirit; impertinent interference; folly; — adj. Excessive, extravagant, exorbitant; redundant, superfluous, exuberant; unnecessary, needless, useless
more: adj. Extraneous; needless; de trop ( fāzil , 1). 2. Useless ( birthā ). fuzūl bāteṅ, fuzūl-goī , n. f. Useless talk; nonsense ( bok ). fuzūl-kharch , adj. Extravagant; prodigal. fuzūl-kharch , n. m. A spendthrift ( uṛāū ). fuzūl-kharchī , n. f. 1. Excess of expenditure. 2. Dissipation; prodigality; waste; extravagance. fuzūl-kharchī k. , v. a. To lavish ( uṛānā , 11). fuzūl-go ; H. bātūnī , n. m. A prolix speaker. fuzūl hai! It is superfluous or unnecessary.

adj. Exuberant, excessive, extravagant, redundant. فضول خرچ faẓūl-ḵẖarch, Extravagant, profuse, prodigal. فضول گو faẓūl-go, 1. A prolix speaker, one who talks much, babbler, talkative. 2. A boaster, egotist.
subs: adj. & s.m. Extravagant, prodigal, lavish, profuse; — an extravagant person, a spendthrift
s.f. Excess of expenditure; extravagance, prodigality, lavishness, waste, dissipation
adj. & s.m. Talking much, talkative; prolix; — a prolix speaker; a great talker; a babbler; a boaster, an egotist.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian فُضُول (fuzūl), borrowed from Arabic فُضُول (fuḍūl), from فَضَلَ (faḍala). First attested in c. 1649 as Middle Hindi فضول (fẓvl /⁠fuzūl⁠/).

ety: fuẓūl, q.v.+ī = Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Excess, extravagance, exorbitance; superfluity, redundance, exuberance; — independence; — meddlesomeness, officiousness.
s.m. One who busies himself with that which does not concern him; one who acts without authority, or who is not a commissioned agent, &c.; a busy-body, meddler, an impertinent fellow.
more: s. f. Exuberance, redundance, excess, exorbitance, extravagance.

def: adj. = afzūn, q.v. adj. Increasing; more; greater; manifold, much
more: part. Increasing, many, much: see افزون afzūn.
subs: v.n. To increase, enlarge; to rise (in price).

def: adv. In one's absence ( pīṭ pīchhe ).
adj. 1. Immoveable. 2. Unaccented.
n. m. A Turkish carpet.
n. m. 1. A kind of cloth; a wrapper. 2. ( Arch .) An architrave. galtāṅ pechāṅ , adv. 1. Wheel within wheel; confusion worse confounded. 2. Absorbed in thought; in a brown study.
n. f. The act of destroying; pillage; destruction.
adj. Sunk; drowned. garqā'nā , H. galānā , v. n. To sink a well.
n. f. Talking; gossip. gauzeṅ laṛāna , v. a. To gossip; gup. gauzī'yah , n. m. A gossip.
adj.
more: (part. act. of غيب Heb. To be obscured or clouded) adj. 1. Absent, away, concealed, invisible, vanished. 2. (substantively) The third person (in gram.). غايب باز g̠ẖāʼịb-bāz, s. m. A conjurer. غاٴيب هونا g̠ẖāʼịb honā, To vanish, to disappear.

def: n. m. 1. Cry; whine. Kyā geṅ peṅ lagā rakkhī hai! 2. Quarrel; fight. Mujh se geṅ peṅ na lānā?
n. m. A bud; rose-bud; blossom. guncha-dahan , Poet. 1. A mouth like a rosebud. 2. A mistress. guncha-i-nā-shuguftah ; H. band kalī , (Slang) A virgin ( bākirah ).
more: adj. Well-populated. Yeh shahar bhī guncha ban rahā hai .
s. m. A bud, rose-bud. غنچه دهن g̠ẖuncha-dahan, With a mouth like a rose-bud, a mistress.

def: n. m. The nasal n .
n. f. Drowsiness ( ūngh ).
ex: اُس پر غنودگی طاری رہتی ہے، اور جب وہ سو جاتا ہے، تو وہ نیند میں بَرّانے لگتا ہے۔
Drowsiness overcomes him, and when he falls asleep, he begins to mutter in his slumber.
wety: From غُنُودَہ (ġunūda, “drowsy”) + ـگی (-gī, “-ness”).

def: 1. The sign of the future tense; will; shall. 2. An honoric form, as Āiye, baiṭhyegā! Come, sit down!
adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Going, moving; — who or what goes (used as a suffix, e.g. kha-ga, vulg. khag, 'going in the air or sky; a bird,' &c.).
s.f. = gav, q.v.; — s.m. = garbh and garv, qq.v.

s.m. The tree Diospyros embryopteris, or D. glutinosa, or Embryopteris glutinifera (the viscid pulp of the ripe fruit is used as gum, in bookbinding; and in place of tar, for paying the seams of boats: the fruit contains much tannin; and the extract of the fruit is an excellent astringent); — pulp, pith, substance; — (dialec.) pregnancy ( = gābh, q.v.).
more: n. m. 1. Embryopteris glutinifera , Wat. The fruit contains a glutinous astringent juice with which the bottoms of boats are smeared, and in which nets are soaked. 2. Pith; substance.

s. m. Name of a tree, the fruit of which contains a glutinous, astringent juice, with which the bottoms of boats are smeared, and in which nets are soaked. (Diospyros glutinosa: Kæn. M.S. Embryopteris glutinifera: Roxb. Pl. Cor. 70.).

def: s.m. = gābhā, q.v. s.m. A new leaf (springing from the centre of a plantain tree), a leaf in the bud; sprout, shoot; calyx (of a flower); unripe crop, half-ripe ear (of corn); inner portion (of wood), liber, alburnum, sap-wood; old cotton (taken out of a quilt, &c.); — fork (of a tree).

ety: inf. n. of غبن 'to forget; to be deficient in judgment,' &c.
def: s.m. Forgetfulness; mistake; weakness of judgment or of mind; — adj. Weak in judgment, or in mind; liable to deception.
s.m. & adv. (colloq.), The sound made in gulping; — gulp-gulp, gobble-gobble.
s.m. = gavāśan, q.v.s.v. gava or gav.

ety: for A. غباوة, inf. n. of غبو 'to have little intelligence,' &c.
def: s.f. Weakness or deficiency of intellect; stupidity; inadvertency.
more: (from غبي To neglect) s. f. Inadvertency, stupidity.

def: adj. Proud, haughty; rich, wealthy, prosperous.
s.m. (dialec.), A bedding.
adj. = gabbar, q.v. adj. Proud, haughty; rich, wealthy, prosperous.
adj. = gābhin, q.v. adj. Pregnant (esp. an animal); — s.f. A pregnant woman; an animal with young.
adj. (f. -ī), Full, filled out, fleshy, plump, stout.
adj. (dialec.) = gabdā, q.v. adj. (f. -ī), Full, filled out, fleshy, plump, stout.
s.m. A dewlap; wattle (of a cock); a double chin
more: s. m. A dewlap, a double chin. چاه غبغب chāh-i-g̠ẖabg̠ẖab, The dimple in the chin.
subs: s.m. A dimple in the chin ( = ćāh-ě-zanaḵẖ).

def: s.m. & adv. (colloq.), The sound made in gulping; — gulp-gulp, gobble-gobble.
more: n. m. The sound made in gulping. Lab-lab pīseṅ, gab-gab khāëṅ, būḍhe bhatār ke bal bal jāëṅ! The corn grind anyhow and gobble all you can! A doating husband old-God bless the dear old man!

def: s.m. Pregnancy (esp. of animals)
subs: To slip or cast a fœtus; to miscarry
The fœtus to be dropped; to miscarry.

def: Sant. n. f. S. गर्भ Pr. gabbh , Pālī, gabbho . Pregnancy (of cattle). gābh ḍālnā, gābh girnā , v. n. To miscarry (cattle).

s.m. A new leaf (springing from the centre of a plantain tree), a leaf in the bud; sprout, shoot; calyx (of a flower); unripe crop, half-ripe ear (of corn); inner portion (of wood), liber, alburnum, sap-wood; old cotton (taken out of a quilt, &c.); — fork (of a tree).

s.m. A new leaf (springing from the centre of a plantain tree), a leaf in the bud; sprout, shoot; calyx (of a flower); unripe crop, half-ripe ear (of corn); inner portion (of wood), liber, alburnum, sap-wood; old cotton (taken out of a quilt, &c.); — fork (of a tree).
more: n. m. 1. A leaf in the bud. Gābhe ke paṅkhe . 2. Unripe crop; half-ripe ears of corn. 3. The inner portion of wood; liber; alburnum; sapwood. 4. A fork of a tree. 5. ( nāmāṅ ) Old cotton taken out of a quilt. gābhe ānā , v. n. To shoot or form (the ears of corn). Bhādoṅ baras kare utrāī, jān Sāvnī gābhe āī . Rus. Plenteous showers in September, a bumper crop reaped in November.
s. m. A new leaf springing from the centre of a plantain tree.

def: n. f. Land contiguous to a village.
s.f. A ray (of light), sunbeam, moon-beam.
s.m. The membrane in which the fœtus is enveloped (syn. kherī).
adj. Pregnant (esp. an animal); — s.f. A pregnant woman; an animal with young.
more: Sant. adj. S. गर्भिणी; Pr. gābbhin ; Pregnant (cattle). Āī na gaī, kaule lag-ke gābhin bhaī . Prov. Nor came nor went she any where, yet pregnant grew just standing there! (She cant say how it happened). gābh'in , E.; gyābhan ; W., n. f. 1. A cow or other mammal with calf. 2. ( Rare ) A pregnant woman. Uske rāj meṅ gābhan gābh ḍāle . Prov. In his reign pregnant women miscarried. (Through fear, as of Nādir Shāh). gyābhan karānā , v. a. To cross (cattle).

def: adj. = gambhīr, and gahirā, qq.v. adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Deep (lit. and fig.); deep-sounding, hollow-toned; profound, sagacious; grave, sedate, serious, solemn; earnest; secret, mysterious; — thick, dense, impervious, impenetrable; inscrutable; — a serious person, &c.; — a large boil on the back (?).
v.t. To snub, chide.
adj. & s.m. = gabrū, q.v. adj. & s.m. Childish, puerile, juvenile; — stout and comely; — a boy, lad, stripling, a beardless youth; an unformed youth, a clown, a lout; — a bridegroom
s.m. (rustic) The hair of a new-born child.

ety: rel. n. fr. غبا, rt. غبو 'to lack intelligence'
def: adj. Having little or no intelligence, weak in mind; ignorant; stupid, dull; — unmindful, forgetful, neglectful, heedless, inadvertent.
more: adj. Weak in mind; dull; muddle-headed. 2. Forgetful.
(from غبو) adj. Weak in mind, forgetful, negligent, imprudent.

def: adj. = gābhin, q.v. adj. Pregnant (esp. an animal); — s.f. A pregnant woman; an animal with young.

ety: act. part. of غبر 'to remain, last,' &c.
def: part. adj. & s.m. Remaining, lasting, continuing; the later; the relic; the remainder; — passing, passing away; past.
more: (part. act. of غبر) adj. 1. Left, remained. 2. Passing away.

ety: inf. n. of غبن 'to cheat, deceive, defraud,' &c.
def: s.m. Cheating, imposing upon, defrauding, &c.; deceit, fraud, imposition; loss (of money, &c.); misappropriation
more: n. m. Misappropriation of money; unlawful abstraction ( khurd burd ). gaban-i-fāsh . A gross fraud. gaban k. , v. a. To misappropriate ( khayānat k .).

....گُڑیا رے گُڑیا تیرا گُڈّا پَردیسِیا جوڑی آسمانی ہوگی شَگُن پِہ دیکھو شادمانی ہوگی ...

more: n. m. gadhī , n. f. S. गर्दभ; Pr. gaḍḍho ; Pālī, gadrabho . 1. An ass; a donkey. 2. A fool; stupid; simpleton. 1. Kābul meṅ kyā gadhe nahīṅ hote? Prov. Are there no donkeys in Kābul then? (Said to one who boasts of his native country). 2. Gadhā pīṭe ghoṛā nahīṅ hotā! Prov. If you bray an ass you will not make a horse of him. (Bruise a fool in a mortar, yet he will not be wise). 3. Gadhoṅ se hal chale to bail kaun bisāve . Prov. If donkeys ploughs could draw who'd oxen buy? 4. Ikkā, vākīl, gadhā, Paṭnā shahar meṅ sadhā . Prov. Ikkas , pleaders, and donkeys abound in Patna. 5. Gadhe ke khilāë kā pāp na pun . Prov. To feed a donkey will take you neither to Hell nor to Heaven. 6. Gadhe ko pūrī aur halvā ( khushkā yā gulqand ). Prov. Cakes and sugar plums for an ass! (Casting pearls before swine). 7. Gadhā barsāt meṅ bhūkā mare! Prov. A donkey starve in the Rains! 8. Gadhā kharsā meṅ moṭā hot…

r: gāf, ga, kāf-ě-ʻajamī, kāf, g, go, ka-varg, abjad, ka, ga-kār
ety: it being unknown in the Arabic
def: s.m. The letter or the sound ga.
more: the 26th letter of the Persian alphabet, does not occur in Arabic. Its sound is that of g in good . In Sanskrit, its corresponding letter is ग In Abjad , q. v. it stands for 20. It is interchangeable with b, t, j, d, ḍ, r, s, /?/g, k , q. v., As an initial, medial, and final with m as ghāīṅ=māīṅ side; gūchh=mūchh moustache; H. gadlā = E. muddy; miry; thāṅgnā = thāmnā hold up; Farrukh. nigaṭnā = nimaṭnā to be settled; sigaṭnā = simaṭnā to collect; ugasnā = umasnā to rot; jug jug = jam jam always; P. laṅg = E. lame. As an initial and final, with v as P. gul = P. vul rose; P. garm = E. warm; E. guard = E. ward; S. taḍāg = H. talāo tank. As a medial with h , as sagodrā = sahodrā own brother; H. jagat = P. jahāṅ world: with y as shigāl = siyāl jackal; P. zar-gūṅ = P. zar-yūṅ golden: with l , as ḍagrā = ḍalyā a basket; agrānā = alhānā rejoice.

the twenty-sixth letter of the Persian alphabet, does not occur in Arabic. Its sound is that of g in good. In Sanskrit, its corresponding letter is ग , and to represent the aspirated घ , it is combined with ه thus گهہ. In the notation of numbers by the letters of the alphabet, it stands for the same as ک. It is changed (1) into ج arabistically or otherwise; as جلنار for گلنار The flower of the pomegranate; لجام for لگام: (2) into د as اورند for اورنگ A throne, pomp, &c. (3) into غ as غاو for گاو A cow; غلوله for گلوله A cotton or pellet ball: (4) into ق (arabistically) as سرقين for سرگين Cow-dung: (5) into خ as فرسخ for فرسنگ A measure of length. It is, moreover, substituted for the silent ه or hā-i-muḵẖtafī at the end of nouns, when the derivatives in ي and the plurals of men or animals are formed; as, ديوانگي Madness, and ديوانگان The mad, from ديوانه. ety: gāh˚ = Prk. गंह(इ) = S. गृह्ना (ति), rt. ग्रह्
def: v.t. To caulk, thresh, tread out (corn); — to catch, take, seize, stop; — to search, seek, inquire into, investigate.
more: v. a. 1. To calk, to thrash, to tread. 2. To inquire, to search, to seek. گاه گاه gāhi gāhi, plup. part. Having inquired or sought repeatedly.

ety: gah˚ = Prk. गंह(इ), or गेंह(इ) = S. गृहा(ति), rt. ग्रह्
def: v.t. To seize, catch, lay hold of; to take; to handle; to put on; — v.n. To be eclipsed (the sun or moon); — to blush.
more: 1. v. a. To lay hold of, to handle, to seize, to catch, to take. 2. To blush. 3. s. m. Jewels, ornaments. 4. Pledge, pawn.
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi گہنا (ghna /⁠gahnā⁠/) (first attested in c. 1697),, from Prakrit 𑀕𑀳𑀦 (gahana) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓- (-ka-), from Sanskrit ग्र॑हण (gràhaṇa, “holding, putting on”). Compare later Sanskrit गहना (gahanā, “ornament”), a borrowing from Middle Indo-Aryan.

def: v. a. S. गाह to move. 1. To thrash or tread out corn. Na koī tālib huā hamārā, na ham ne dil se kisī ko chāhā, Na ham ne dekhī khushī kī lahreṅ, na dard o gam se kabhī karāhā, Na ham ne boyā, na ham ne jotā, na ham ne kāṭā, na ham ne gāhā, Uṭhā jo dil se bharam kā pardah to uske uṭhte hī phir, ahā! hā! Na bāp, beṭā, na dost, dushman, na āshaq o yeh sanam kīsī ke: Ajab tarah kī huī farāgat, na koī hamārā, na ham kisī ke! Nazīr. Sought by no one, I gave my heart to none, Nor extasy nor pangs of grief I've known, Nor ploughed, nor sowed, nor reaped, nor threshed the sown, The film dropped from my eyes—Oh! joy unknown! Father, or son, friend, foe, or mistress none, Such freedom rare! none mine, owned by no one! 2. To search; seek ( ḍhūṅḍhnā ).

s.m. An ornament, a trinket, a jewel; jewels, jewellery, trinkets; — a pledge, anything in pledge, pawn; mortgage; — a heavy plank (or flat piece of wood) on which a man stands while it is dragged by oxen over a ploughed field to level the ground (syn. paṭṛā); a kind of harrow without teeth
subs: (-ko), To place or put in pawn; to pawn, pledge.
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi گہنا (ghna /⁠gahnā⁠/) (first attested in c. 1697),, from Prakrit 𑀕𑀳𑀦 (gahana) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓- (-ka-), from Sanskrit ग्र॑हण (gràhaṇa, “holding, putting on”). Compare later Sanskrit गहना (gahanā, “ornament”), a borrowing from Middle Indo-Aryan.

def: n. m. S. गहन A harrow without teeth.
more: n. m. S. ग्रहण 1. ( gahnā pātā ) Jewels; jewellery; ornaments. 1. Gahnā pahan-kar ḍhak chal, pūt jan-kar niv chal . Wom. Prov. In jewels decked veiled walk, though you've a son yet humbly walk! 2. An-gahnī kā gahnā . A jewel by her wore who jewels never wore before. 3. Gahnā ek kanak te gahnā, wā meṅ bhāo na dūjā , Kabīr. Kahan sunan ke dui kar thāpe, ek nimāj, ek pūjā . Though jewels various forms may wear, The gold's the same that makes all fair, Though two they seem to tougue and ear, One prayer, one worship only here. 2. A pledge; pawn. gahnā-patr , n. m. A deed of mortgage. gahnā chaṛhānā , v. a. To present jewels to the bride. gahne rakhnā , v. a. To pawn jewels. phūloṅ kā gahnā . Wreaths of flowers.

v. a. S. ग्रहण; Pr. geṅhā take. 1. To lay hold of; catch ( pakaṛnā , 1—3). Bāṭ chalat mero achrā gahe, Merī sune, na apnī kahe, Nā kuchh mo sūṅ jhagṛā jhāṅṭā: 'Kyoṅ sakhī, sajjan?' 'Nā, sakhī, kāṅṭā .' Riddle. Mukrī . My garment's hem he seizes on the way, He hears me not, nor yet a word will say, No quarrel with him had, nor hard words borne; 'A lover is it, dear?' 'No, dear, a thorn.' 2. To be eclipsed.

def: s.f. A deformed cow born during an eclipse of the moon; — a small (or undersized) cow.
more: 1. s. f. Oakum. 2. Act of calking. 3. adj. Pledged. گہني دهرنا gahnī dharnā, v. a. To pawn, to pledge.

def: n. f. A deformed cow born during an eclipse of the moon.

def: n. m. 1. A large bundle of sheaves, containing about ten seers of grain. 2. The daily wages in kind of a labourer in harvest time.
more: adj. S. गम्भीर 1. Deep; profound; deep-seated. 2. Strong (liquor). Gahrā nasha . 3. Close; intimate. Gahrā yārānah. gahrā raṅg , n. m. A deep color. gahrā kāṭā , ( Bot .) Laciniate. gahrā'nā , v. a. To deepen; make deep. gahrā'o , n. m. gahrāī , n. f. Depth; profundity; cavity. gahrāo ; A. mujavvaf , ( Geom .) Concave. gahrī bāt, gahrā khayāl . A deep thought; an important or weighty observation. gahre chalo , ( Ṭhags ) Murder the traveller! gahrī chhannā , v. n. 1. To be close friends. 2. To have a great fight. gahrī gintī . Algebra. gahrī gintī joṛ . An Algebrical sum. gahrī nīṅd , n. f. Sound sleep. Gāḍī ke gaḍvāliye, gahrī nīṅd na so! Paṛā rahegā bāṭ meṅ, tere bail jāëṅge kho . O waggoner! take heed, sleep not, my friend too sound! Upon the road you 'll lie, your cattle nowhere found. gahre h. , v. n. To gain; thrive; fatten.

n. Treading out corn. Dhartī ghāl tujhāṅ yūṅ rauṅdhūṅ, rauṅdheṅ gāhṭā jūṅ dhorī, kit chālī āj siṅgar, gorī! W. Rus. song. I'll throw and crush you on the ground, As oxen corn tread in their round, Where, wife, bedecked are you now bound?
